items.title: The Masc–PSI complex directly induces male-type doublesex splicing in silkworms
items.author: Susumu Katsuma
items.content: The Masc–PSI protein complex binds to female-specific doublesex regions, inducing exon skipping, and thereby promoting male-type doublesex expression in silkworm males.

items.title: Genetic diversity and comparative genomics across Leishmania (Viannia) species
items.author: Jorge Duitama
items.content: Population genomics and chromosome-level genome assemblies for Leishmania strains circulating in Colombia reveal population structure, copy number variation and evolution of amastin families.

items.title: Doubling multiplexed imaging capability via spatial expression pattern-guided protein pairing and computational unmixing
items.author: Young-Gyu Yoon
items.content: Spatial expression pattern-guided pairing and computational unmixing of co-labeled protein signals enable volumetric multiplexed imaging with fewer fluorophores, effectively doubling multiplexing capacity.

items.title: Mucosal immune responses and intestinal microbiome associations in wild spotted hyenas (Crocuta crocuta)
items.author: Susana C. M. Ferreira
items.content: In wild spotted hyenas, the gut microbiome is shaped by mucosal immunity. Mucosal immune measures have strong associations with specific taxa of bacteria, hookworms and fungi known to establish mutualistic, commensal and parasitic interactions.

items.title: Sperm navigation in humans: a concerted action of multiple means
items.author: Michael Eisenbach
items.content: Sperm navigate the human Fallopian tube by three distinct processes. While their behavioral mechanisms are known, their molecular basis is gradually being uncovered. These processes can improve in vitro fertilization outcomes in clinical settings.

items.title: Cocaine-induced gene regulation in D1 and D2 neuronal ensembles of the nucleus accumbens
items.author: Eric J. Nestler
items.content: Single-nucleus RNA-seq reveals how cocaine reshapes gene programs in D1 and D2 medium spiny neurons of the mouse nucleus accumbens, revealing cell-type-specific plasticity pathways.

items.title: Evaluating analytic strategies to obtain high-resolution, vertex-level measures of cortical neuroanatomy in children in low- and middle-income countries
items.author: Christine Ecker
items.content: Study in children in LMIC evaluates analytic strategies to obtain high-resolution, vertex-level measures of cortical neuroanatomy using low-field magnetic resonance imaging.

items.title: Forward and convergent remapping of receptive fields from local field potentials in frontal eye fields
items.author: Mingsha Zhang
items.content: In frontal eye fields, the alpha receptive fields (RFs) constantly showed forward remapping under modulation of corollary discharges (CD), whereas the high-gamma RFs showed dynamic changes between convergent and forward remapping under the modulation of attention and CD.
